87	/// Commit all pending WGMMA operations to a group.
88	///
89	/// After issuing one or more `wgmma_mma_*` instructions, call this to
90	/// commit them as a group. The group can then be waited on with
91	/// `wgmma_wait_group`.
92	///
93	/// # PTX
94	///
95	/// ```ptx
96	/// wgmma.commit_group.sync.aligned;
97	/// ```
98	#[inline(never)]
99	pub fn wgmma_commit_group() {
100	    // Lowered to: call void @llvm.nvvm.wgmma.commit_group.sync.aligned()
101	    unreachable!("wgmma_commit_group called outside CUDA kernel context")
102	}
103	
104	/// Wait for WGMMA groups to complete.
105	///
106	/// Waits until at most N groups of WGMMA operations are still pending.
107	/// Use N=0 to wait for ALL pending groups to complete.
108	///
109	/// # Type Parameters
110	///
111	/// - `N`: Maximum number of pending groups allowed (0-7). Use 0 to wait for all.
112	///
113	/// # PTX
114	///
115	/// ```ptx
116	/// wgmma.wait_group.sync.aligned N;
117	/// ```
118	///
119	/// # Example
120	///
121	/// ```rust,ignore
122	/// // Issue multiple WGMMA groups
123	/// wgmma_mma_m64n64k16_f32_bf16(&mut acc, desc_a1, desc_b1);
124	/// wgmma_commit_group();
125	///
126	/// wgmma_mma_m64n64k16_f32_bf16(&mut acc, desc_a2, desc_b2);
127	/// wgmma_commit_group();
128	///
129	/// // Wait for all to complete
130	/// wgmma_wait_group::<0>();
131	/// ```
132	#[inline(never)]
133	pub fn wgmma_wait_group<const N: u32>() {
134	    let _ = N;
135	    // Lowered to: call void @llvm.nvvm.wgmma.wait_group.sync.aligned(i64 N)
136	    unreachable!("wgmma_wait_group called outside CUDA kernel context")
137	}

139	// =============================================================================
140	// SMEM Descriptor Creation
141	// =============================================================================

143	/// Create a 64-bit shared memory descriptor for WGMMA input matrices.
144	///
145	/// This descriptor tells the tensor core hardware how to interpret the
146	/// matrix data in shared memory. It encodes:
147	/// - Base address in shared memory
148	/// - Leading dimension (stride between rows/columns)
149	/// - Overall stride
150	/// - Swizzling mode (128-byte swizzle for bank conflict avoidance)
151	///
152	/// # Parameters
153	///
154	/// - `ptr`: Pointer to matrix data in shared memory
155	///
156	/// # Returns
157	///
158	/// A 64-bit descriptor suitable for WGMMA instructions.
159	///
160	/// # Descriptor Layout
161	///
162	/// ```text
163	/// Bits [0:17]   - Base address (shifted right by 4)
164	/// Bits [16:33]  - Leading dimension (shifted right by 4)
165	/// Bits [32:49]  - Stride (shifted right by 4)
166	/// Bit  [62]     - 128-byte swizzle enable
167	/// ```
168	///
169	/// # Safety
170	///
171	/// - `ptr` must point to valid shared memory
172	/// - The memory layout must match WGMMA requirements (proper alignment, swizzling)
173	///
174	/// # PTX
175	///
176	/// Uses `cvta.shared.u32` to convert generic pointer to shared memory address.
177	#[inline(never)]
178	pub unsafe fn make_smem_desc(ptr: *const u8) -> u64 {
179	    let _ = ptr;
180	    // Lowered to inline PTX:
181	    // {
182	    //   .reg .u32 addr;
183	    //   .reg .u64 desc;
184	    //   cvta.shared.u32 addr, %ptr;
185	    //   shr.u32 addr, addr, 4;
186	    //   and.b32 addr, addr, 0x3FFFF;
187	    //   cvt.u64.u32 desc, addr;
188	    //   ... (encode leading dim, stride, swizzle)
189	    //   mov.u64 %result, desc;
190	    // }
191	    unreachable!("make_smem_desc called outside CUDA kernel context")
192	}
193	
194	/// Create an SMEM descriptor with custom leading dimension and stride.

198	/// # Parameters
199	///
200	/// - `ptr`: Pointer to matrix data in shared memory
201	/// - `leading_dim`: Leading dimension in bytes (divided by 16 internally)
202	/// - `stride`: Stride in bytes (divided by 16 internally)
203	/// - `swizzle_128b`: Enable 128-byte swizzling
204	///
205	/// # Safety
206	///
207	/// - `ptr` must be a valid pointer to matrix data in shared memory
208	/// - Must be called from within a CUDA kernel context
209	#[inline(never)]
210	pub unsafe fn make_smem_desc_custom(
211	    ptr: *const u8,
212	    leading_dim: u32,
213	    stride: u32,
214	    swizzle_128b: bool,
215	) -> u64 {
216	    let _ = (ptr, leading_dim, stride, swizzle_128b);
217	    unreachable!("make_smem_desc_custom called outside CUDA kernel context")
218	}

220	// =============================================================================
221	// WGMMA Matrix Multiply-Accumulate Instructions
222	// =============================================================================
223	
224	/// Warpgroup matrix multiply-accumulate: D += A × B
225	///
226	/// Performs a 64×64×16 matrix multiplication using tensor cores at the
227	/// warpgroup level. All 128 threads in the warpgroup participate.
228	///
229	/// # Matrix Dimensions
230	///
231	/// - **A**: 64×16 (M=64 rows, K=16 cols), row-major in shared memory
232	/// - **B**: 16×64 (K=16 rows, N=64 cols), column-major in shared memory
233	/// - **D**: 64×64 output, accumulated in registers
234	///
235	/// # Accumulator Layout
236	///
237	/// The 64×64 output is distributed across 128 threads:
238	/// - Each thread holds 32 floats in `[[f32; 8]; 4]`
239	/// - 128 threads × 32 = 4096 floats = 64×64
240	///
241	/// # Parameters
242	///
243	/// - `acc`: Mutable reference to the accumulator (32 floats per thread)
244	/// - `desc_a`: SMEM descriptor for matrix A (from `make_smem_desc`)
245	/// - `desc_b`: SMEM descriptor for matrix B (from `make_smem_desc`)
246	///
247	/// # PTX
248	///
249	/// ```ptx
250	/// wgmma.mma_async.sync.aligned.m64n64k16.f32.bf16.bf16
251	///     {%f0, %f1, ..., %f31}, %rd_desc_a, %rd_desc_b,
252	///     1, 1, 1, 0, 0;
253	/// ```
254	///
255	/// # Safety
256	///
257	/// - Descriptors must be valid SMEM descriptors
258	/// - Must be called by all threads in a warpgroup
259	/// - Must be called from within a CUDA kernel context on sm_90a+
260	///
261	/// # Example
262	///
263	/// ```rust,ignore
264	/// // Process a 64×64 K-tile (requires 4 WGMMA calls since K=16 per call)
265	/// for k in 0..4 {
266	///     let offset = k * 16 * elem_size;
267	///     wgmma_mma_m64n64k16_f32_bf16(
268	///         &mut acc,
269	///         desc_a + offset as u64,
270	///         desc_b + offset as u64,
271	///     );
272	/// }
273	/// wgmma_commit_group();
274	/// wgmma_wait_group::<0>();
275	/// ```
276	#[inline(never)]
277	pub unsafe fn wgmma_mma_m64n64k16_f32_bf16(acc: &mut [[f32; 8]; 4], desc_a: u64, desc_b: u64) {
278	    let _ = (acc, desc_a, desc_b);
279	    // Lowered to inline PTX with 32 accumulator registers + 2 descriptors + 5 immediates
280	    unreachable!("wgmma_mma_m64n64k16_f32_bf16 called outside CUDA kernel context")
281	}
282	
283	/// WGMMA with f32 accumulator and f16 (IEEE half) inputs.
284	///
285	/// Same as `wgmma_mma_m64n64k16_f32_bf16` but uses f16 input format.
286	///
287	/// # Safety
288	///
289	/// - Descriptors must be valid SMEM descriptors
290	/// - Must be called by all threads in a warpgroup
291	/// - Must be called from within a CUDA kernel context on sm_90a+
292	#[inline(never)]
293	pub unsafe fn wgmma_mma_m64n64k16_f32_f16(acc: &mut [[f32; 8]; 4], desc_a: u64, desc_b: u64) {
294	    let _ = (acc, desc_a, desc_b);
295	    unreachable!("wgmma_mma_m64n64k16_f32_f16 called outside CUDA kernel context")
296	}
297	
298	/// WGMMA with f32 accumulator and tf32 (TensorFloat-32) inputs.
299	///
300	/// TF32 uses 19 bits (1 sign + 8 exponent + 10 mantissa), providing
301	/// better precision than bf16 while maintaining tensor core throughput.
302	///
303	/// # Safety
304	///
305	/// - Descriptors must be valid SMEM descriptors
306	/// - Must be called by all threads in a warpgroup
307	/// - Must be called from within a CUDA kernel context on sm_90a+
308	#[inline(never)]
309	pub unsafe fn wgmma_mma_m64n64k16_f32_tf32(acc: &mut [[f32; 8]; 4], desc_a: u64, desc_b: u64) {
310	    let _ = (acc, desc_a, desc_b);
311	    unreachable!("wgmma_mma_m64n64k16_f32_tf32 called outside CUDA kernel context")
312	}
313	
314	// =============================================================================
315	// Accumulator Utilities
316	// =============================================================================
317	
318	/// Type alias for the WGMMA accumulator (m64n64 tile, 32 floats per thread).
319	pub type Acc64x64 = [[f32; 8]; 4];
320	
321	/// Initialize an accumulator to zero.
322	///
323	/// # Returns
324	///
325	/// A zeroed accumulator suitable for WGMMA operations.
326	#[inline(always)]
327	pub const fn zero_accumulator() -> Acc64x64 {
328	    [[0.0f32; 8]; 4]
329	}
330

content.content:
1	/*
2	 * SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) 2026 NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
3	 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
4	 */
5	
6	//! Tensor Core Gen 5 (tcgen05) for Blackwell architectures (sm_100+).
7	//!
8	//! tcgen05 is Blackwell's tensor core instruction set, replacing WGMMA from Hopper.
9	//! The key architectural change is **single-thread MMA semantics** - one thread can
10	//! issue an entire matrix multiply operation.
11	//!
12	//! # Key Differences from WGMMA
13	//!
14	//! | Aspect | WGMMA (Hopper) | tcgen05 (Blackwell) |
15	//! |--------|---------------|---------------------|
16	//! | MMA issue | 128 threads collectively | **1 thread** |
17	//! | Matrix A/D storage | Registers/SMEM | **Tensor Memory (TMEM)** |
18	//! | Allocation | Implicit | **Dynamic (tcgen05.alloc)** |
19	//! | Wait mechanism | `wgmma.wait_group` | **mbarrier.try_wait** |
20	//!
21	//! # Tensor Memory (TMEM)
22	//!
23	//! TMEM is a new per-SM memory type for tensor operands:
24	//! - Dynamically allocated at runtime
25	//! - Allocation unit: 32 columns (range: 32-512)
26	//! - One WARP must perform allocation (warp-synchronous)
27	//! - Must be explicitly deallocated before kernel exits
28	//!
29	//! # Thread Semantics
30	//!
31	//! ```text
32	//! ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
33	//! │              Thread Requirements by Operation                        │
34	//! ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
35	//! │  tcgen05_mma_ws:    █         (1 thread)                            │
36	//! │  tcgen05_commit:    █         (1 thread)                            │
37	//! │  tcgen05_fence:     █         (1 thread)                            │
38	//! │  tcgen05_alloc:     ████████  (32 threads / 1 warp)                 │
39	//! │  tcgen05_dealloc:   ████████  (32 threads / 1 warp)                 │
40	//! └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
41	//! ```
42	//!
43	//! # Usage Pattern
44	//!
45	//! ```rust,ignore
46	//! use cuda_device::tcgen05::*;
47	//! use cuda_device::barrier::*;
48	//!
49	//! // 1. Allocate TMEM (warp-synchronous - all 32 threads in warp)
50	//! if warp_id() == 0 {
51	//!     tcgen05_alloc(tmem_addr_ptr, 64);  // 64 columns
52	//! }
53	//! sync_threads();
54	//!
55	//! // 2. Single thread issues MMA
56	//! if thread_id() == 0 {
57	//!     tcgen05_fence_before_thread_sync();
58	//!     tcgen05_mma_ws_f16(d_tmem, a_tmem, a_desc, b_desc, idesc, false);
59	//!     tcgen05_commit(&mbar);
60	//! }
61	//!
62	//! // 3. All threads wait via mbarrier
63	//! mbarrier_try_wait(&mbar, 0);
64	//!
65	//! // 4. Deallocate TMEM (warp-synchronous)
66	//! if warp_id() == 0 {
67	//!     tcgen05_dealloc(tmem_addr, 64);
68	//! }
69	//! ```
70	//!
71	//! # Hardware Support
72	//!
73	//! - **sm_100/sm_100a**: B100, B200 (Data Center)
74	//! - **sm_120/sm_120a**: RTX 5090 (Consumer)
75	
76	use core::marker::PhantomData;
77	
78	// =============================================================================
79	// Tensor Memory (TMEM) Types
80	// =============================================================================
81	
82	/// Handle to allocated Tensor Memory.
83	///
84	/// TMEM addresses are 32-bit values returned by `tcgen05_alloc`. The address
85	/// is written to shared memory by the allocation instruction.
86	///
87	/// # Note
88	///
89	/// This is NOT a Rust-managed allocation. You must explicitly call
90	/// `tcgen05_dealloc` before the kernel exits.
91	#[repr(transparent)]
92	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
93	pub struct TensorMemoryHandle {
94	    /// The 32-bit TMEM address
95	    pub addr: u32,
96	}
97	
98	impl TensorMemoryHandle {
99	    /// Create a handle from a raw TMEM address.
100	    ///
101	    /// # Safety
102	    ///
103	    /// The address must have been returned by `tcgen05_alloc`.
104	    #[inline(always)]
105	    pub const unsafe fn from_raw(addr: u32) -> Self {
106	        Self { addr }
107	    }
108	
109	    /// Get the raw TMEM address.
110	    #[inline(always)]
111	    pub const fn raw(self) -> u32 {
112	        self.addr
113	    }
114	}
115	
116	// =============================================================================
117	// TMEM Guard with Typestate (Managed TMEM Lifecycle)
118	// =============================================================================
119	
120	/// State marker: TMEM slot not yet allocated.
121	pub struct TmemUninit;
122	
123	/// State marker: TMEM is allocated and ready to use.
124	pub struct TmemReady;
125	
126	/// State marker: TMEM has been deallocated.
127	pub struct TmemDeallocated;
128	
129	/// Newtype for TMEM addresses with type safety.
130	///
131	/// Prevents accidentally using raw u32 values as TMEM addresses.
132	#[repr(transparent)]
133	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
134	pub struct TmemAddress(u32);
135	
136	impl TmemAddress {
137	    /// Get the raw TMEM address value.
138	    #[inline(always)]
139	    pub const fn raw(self) -> u32 {
140	        self.0
141	    }
142	
143	    /// Create from raw value (for internal/advanced use).
144	    ///
145	    /// # Safety
146	    ///
147	    /// The value must be a valid TMEM address from `tcgen05_alloc`.
148	    #[inline(always)]
149	    pub const unsafe fn from_raw(addr: u32) -> Self {
150	        Self(addr)
151	    }
152	}
153	
154	/// Managed TMEM allocation with typestate lifecycle.
155	///
156	/// This type provides compile-time safety for TMEM allocation/deallocation:
157	/// - Cannot use TMEM before allocation
158	/// - Cannot allocate twice
159	/// - Cannot use after deallocation
160	///
161	/// # Type Parameters
162	///
163	/// - `State`: Lifecycle state (`TmemUninit`, `TmemReady`, `TmemDeallocated`)
164	/// - `N_COLS`: Number of columns (must be power of 2: 32, 64, 128, 256, 512)
165	///
166	/// # Thread Requirements
167	///
168	/// TMEM allocation/deallocation is **warp-synchronous**:
169	/// - All 32 threads in the allocating warp must participate
170	/// - Other warps do nothing during alloc/dealloc
171	///
172	/// The `alloc()` and `dealloc()` methods handle this automatically using the
173	/// "all threads call, designated warp executes" pattern.
174	///
175	/// # Example
176	///
177	/// ```rust,ignore
178	/// // Declare shared memory slot for TMEM address storage
179	/// static mut TMEM_SLOT: SharedArray<u32, 1, 4> = SharedArray::UNINIT;
180	///
181	/// // ALL threads create Uninit handle
182	/// let tmem = TmemGuard::<TmemUninit, 512>::from_static(&raw mut TMEM_SLOT as *mut u32);
183	///
184	/// // ALL threads call alloc - only warp 0 actually allocates
185	/// let tmem = unsafe { tmem.alloc() };  // Returns TmemReady
186	///
187	/// // Get the address for use in MMA operations
188	/// let addr = tmem.address();
189	///
190	/// // ... use TMEM in MMA operations ...
191	///
192	/// // ALL threads call dealloc - only warp 0 actually deallocates
193	/// let _dead = unsafe { tmem.dealloc() };  // Returns TmemDeallocated
194	/// ```
195	pub struct TmemGuard<State, const N_COLS: u32> {
196	    /// Pointer to shared memory where the TMEM address is stored
197	    smem_ptr: *mut u32,
198	    _state: PhantomData<State>,
199	}
200	
201	// Safety: Pointer is only accessed through synchronized operations
202	unsafe impl<S, const N: u32> Send for TmemGuard<S, N> {}
203	
204	impl<const N_COLS: u32> TmemGuard<TmemUninit, N_COLS> {
205	    /// Create an uninitialized TMEM guard from an explicit static declaration.
206	    ///
207	    /// The shared memory slot will be used by `tcgen05_alloc` to store the
208	    /// allocated TMEM address.
209	    ///
210	    /// # Example
211	    ///
212	    /// ```rust,ignore
213	    /// static mut TMEM_SLOT: SharedArray<u32, 1, 4> = SharedArray::UNINIT;
214	    ///
215	    /// let tmem = TmemGuard::<TmemUninit, 512>::from_static(&raw mut TMEM_SLOT as *mut u32);
216	    /// ```
217	    #[inline(always)]
218	    pub fn from_static(smem_ptr: *mut u32) -> Self {
219	        TmemGuard {
220	            smem_ptr,
221	            _state: PhantomData,
222	        }
223	    }
224	
225	    /// Allocate TMEM columns.
226	    ///
227	    /// **All threads in the block should call this.** Only warp 0 performs
228	    /// the actual allocation; all threads synchronize and receive a `TmemReady` handle.
229	    ///
230	    /// This is a convenience wrapper for `alloc_by(0)`.
231	    ///
232	    /// # Safety
233	    ///
234	    /// - Must be called before any TMEM operations
235	    /// - All participating threads in the block must call this together
236	    /// - N_COLS must be a power of 2 in range [32, 512]
237	    ///
238	    /// # Example
239	    ///
240	    /// ```rust,ignore
241	    /// // ALL threads call alloc - only warp 0 actually allocates
242	    /// let tmem = unsafe { tmem.alloc() };
243	    /// ```
244	    #[inline(always)]
245	    pub unsafe fn alloc(self) -> TmemGuard<TmemReady, N_COLS> {
246	        unsafe { self.alloc_by(0) }
247	    }
248	
249	    /// Allocate TMEM columns with a specific warp performing allocation.
250	    ///
251	    /// **All threads in the block should call this.** Only threads in the
252	    /// designated warp perform the actual allocation; all threads synchronize
253	    /// and receive a `TmemReady` handle.
254	    ///
255	    /// # Parameters
256	    ///
257	    /// - `alloc_warp`: Warp ID that performs allocation (all 32 threads in this warp)
258	    ///
259	    /// # Safety
260	    ///
261	    /// - Must be called before any TMEM operations
262	    /// - All participating threads in the block must call this together
263	    /// - `alloc_warp` must be a valid warp ID within the block
264	    ///
265	    /// # Example
266	    ///
267	    /// ```rust,ignore
268	    /// // Use warp 1 for allocation
269	    /// let tmem = unsafe { tmem.alloc_by(1) };
270	    /// ```
271	    #[inline(always)]
272	    pub unsafe fn alloc_by(self, alloc_warp: u32) -> TmemGuard<TmemReady, N_COLS> {
273	        let warp_id = crate::warp::warp_id();
274	
275	        if warp_id == alloc_warp {
276	            unsafe { tcgen05_alloc(self.smem_ptr, N_COLS) };
277	        }
278	
279	        // All threads synchronize - ensures allocation is complete
280	        crate::thread::sync_threads();
281	
282	        TmemGuard {
283	            smem_ptr: self.smem_ptr,
284	            _state: PhantomData,
285	        }
286	    }
287	}
288	
289	impl<const N_COLS: u32> TmemGuard<TmemReady, N_COLS> {
290	    /// Get the allocated TMEM address.
291	    ///
292	    /// This reads the address that `tcgen05_alloc` wrote to shared memory.
293	    ///
294	    /// # Note
295	    ///
296	    /// All threads in the block can call this - they all read the same value.
297	    #[inline(always)]
298	    pub fn address(&self) -> TmemAddress {
299	        unsafe { TmemAddress(*self.smem_ptr) }
300	    }
301	
302	    /// Get the raw TMEM address value.
303	    ///
304	    /// Convenience method for passing to intrinsics that take raw u32.
305	    #[inline(always)]
306	    pub fn raw_address(&self) -> u32 {
307	        unsafe { *self.smem_ptr }
308	    }
309	
310	    /// Get the number of allocated columns.
311	    #[inline(always)]
312	    pub const fn n_cols(&self) -> u32 {
313	        N_COLS
314	    }
315	
316	    /// Deallocate the TMEM.
317	    ///
318	    /// **All threads in the block should call this.** Only warp 0 performs
319	    /// the actual deallocation; all threads synchronize before returning.
320	    ///
321	    /// This is a convenience wrapper for `dealloc_by(0)`.
322	    ///
323	    /// Consumes the `TmemReady` guard and returns a `TmemDeallocated` guard.
324	    ///
325	    /// # CRITICAL
326	    ///
327	    /// ALL allocated TMEM MUST be deallocated before the kernel exits.
328	    /// Failure to do so results in `CUDA_ERROR_TENSOR_MEMORY_LEAK`.
329	    ///
330	    /// # Safety
331	    ///
332	    /// - All threads must have completed their TMEM operations before calling
333	    /// - All participating threads in the block must call this together
334	    ///
335	    /// # Example
336	    ///
337	    /// ```rust,ignore
338	    /// // ALL threads call dealloc - only warp 0 actually deallocates
339	    /// let _dead = unsafe { tmem.dealloc() };
340	    /// ```
341	    #[inline(always)]
342	    pub unsafe fn dealloc(self) -> TmemGuard<TmemDeallocated, N_COLS> {
343	        unsafe { self.dealloc_by(0) }
344	    }
345	
346	    /// Deallocate TMEM with a specific warp performing deallocation.
347	    ///
348	    /// **All threads in the block should call this.** Only threads in the
349	    /// designated warp perform the actual deallocation; all threads synchronize
350	    /// before returning.
351	    ///
352	    /// # Parameters
353	    ///
354	    /// - `dealloc_warp`: Warp ID that performs deallocation (must match alloc warp)
355	    ///
356	    /// # Safety
357	    ///
358	    /// - All threads must have completed their TMEM operations before calling
359	    /// - All participating threads in the block must call this together
360	    /// - `dealloc_warp` should typically match the warp that allocated
361	    ///
362	    /// # Example
363	    ///
364	    /// ```rust,ignore
365	    /// // Use warp 1 for deallocation (should match allocation warp)
366	    /// let _dead = unsafe { tmem.dealloc_by(1) };
367	    /// ```
368	    #[inline(always)]
369	    pub unsafe fn dealloc_by(self, dealloc_warp: u32) -> TmemGuard<TmemDeallocated, N_COLS> {
370	        // Ensure all threads are done with TMEM before deallocating
371	        crate::thread::sync_threads();
372	
373	        let warp_id = crate::warp::warp_id();
374	
375	        if warp_id == dealloc_warp {
376	            unsafe {
377	                let tmem_addr = *self.smem_ptr;
378	                tcgen05_dealloc(tmem_addr, N_COLS);
379	            }
380	        }
381	
382	        // All threads synchronize - ensures deallocation is complete
383	        crate::thread::sync_threads();
384	
385	        TmemGuard {
386	            smem_ptr: self.smem_ptr,
387	            _state: PhantomData,
388	        }
389	    }
390	}
391	
392	// TmemGuard<TmemDeallocated, _> has NO methods - can't use after dealloc!
393	
394	// =============================================================================
395	// TMEM Allocation / Deallocation
396	// =============================================================================
397	
398	/// Allocate Tensor Memory columns.
399	///
400	/// This is a **warp-synchronous** operation - ALL 32 threads in the warp must
401	/// execute this instruction together.
402	///
403	/// # How It Works
404	///
405	/// The hardware allocates TMEM and **writes the allocated address back** to the
406	/// shared memory location you provide. You then read this address from shared
407	/// memory to use in subsequent tcgen05 operations:
408	///
409	/// ```text
410	/// ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
411	/// │  tcgen05_alloc(&mut SMEM_ADDR, 64)                                  │
412	/// │                                                                     │
413	/// │  1. You provide: shared memory pointer (SMEM_ADDR)                  │
414	/// │  2. Hardware allocates: 64 columns of TMEM                          │
415	/// │  3. Hardware writes: allocated TMEM address → [SMEM_ADDR]           │
416	/// │  4. You read: tmem_addr = *SMEM_ADDR  (e.g., 0, 64, 128...)         │
417	/// └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
418	/// ```
419	///
420	/// # Parameters
421	///
422	/// - `dst_smem`: Pointer to shared memory where the **hardware will write**
423	///   the allocated TMEM address (output parameter)
424	/// - `n_cols`: Number of columns to allocate (must be power of 2: 32, 64, 128, 256, 512)
425	///
426	/// # Blocking Behavior
427	///
428	/// This instruction blocks if the requested TMEM is not available, and unblocks
429	/// when sufficient TMEM becomes available.
430	///
431	/// # Example
432	///
433	/// ```rust,ignore
434	/// static mut TMEM_ADDR: SharedArray<u32, 1, 4> = SharedArray::UNINIT;
435	///
436	/// // Warp 0 allocates (all 32 threads must participate)
437	/// if warp_id == 0 {
438	///     tcgen05_alloc(&raw mut TMEM_ADDR as *mut u32, 64);
439	/// }
440	/// sync_threads();
441	///
442	/// // Read the allocated address from shared memory
443	/// let tmem_addr = *(&raw const TMEM_ADDR as *const u32);
444	/// // tmem_addr is now the TMEM address (e.g., 0, 64, 128...)
445	/// ```
446	///
447	/// # Safety
448	///
449	/// - Must be called by ALL threads in a warp together
450	/// - `dst_smem` must point to valid shared memory (4-byte aligned)
451	/// - `n_cols` must be a power of 2 in range [32, 512]
452	/// - Allocations should not increase in size between calls (hardware constraint)
453	///
454	/// # PTX
455	///
456	/// ```ptx
457	/// tcgen05.alloc.cta_group::1.sync.aligned.shared::cta.b32 [dst], n_cols;
458	/// ```
459	#[inline(never)]
460	pub unsafe fn tcgen05_alloc(dst_smem: *mut u32, n_cols: u32) {
461	    let _ = (dst_smem, n_cols);
462	    unreachable!("tcgen05_alloc called outside CUDA kernel context")
463	}
464	
465	/// Deallocate Tensor Memory.
466	///
467	/// This is a **warp-synchronous** operation - ALL 32 threads in the warp must
468	/// execute this instruction together.
469	///
470	/// # CRITICAL
471	///
472	/// ALL Tensor Memory allocated with `tcgen05_alloc` MUST be deallocated before
473	/// the kernel exits. Failure to do so results in `CUDA_ERROR_TENSOR_MEMORY_LEAK`.
474	///
475	/// # Parameters
476	///
477	/// - `tmem_addr`: The TMEM address that was **written to shared memory** by
478	///   `tcgen05_alloc`. Read it from shared memory before calling this function.
479	/// - `n_cols`: Number of columns (must match the value used in allocation)
480	///
481	/// # Example
482	///
483	/// ```rust,ignore
484	/// // Read the TMEM address that tcgen05_alloc wrote to shared memory
485	/// let tmem_addr = *(&raw const TMEM_ADDR as *const u32);
486	///
487	/// // Deallocate (warp 0, all 32 threads)
488	/// if warp_id == 0 {
489	///     tcgen05_dealloc(tmem_addr, 64);
490	/// }
491	/// ```
492	///
493	/// # Safety
494	///
495	/// - Must be called by ALL threads in a warp together
496	/// - `tmem_addr` must be a valid address from `tcgen05_alloc`
497	/// - `n_cols` must match the value used in allocation
498	///
499	/// # PTX
500	///
501	/// ```ptx
502	/// tcgen05.dealloc.cta_group::1.sync.aligned.b32 tmem_addr, n_cols;
503	/// ```
504	#[inline(never)]
505	pub unsafe fn tcgen05_dealloc(tmem_addr: u32, n_cols: u32) {
506	    let _ = (tmem_addr, n_cols);
507	    unreachable!("tcgen05_dealloc called outside CUDA kernel context")
508	}
509	
510	/// Relinquish the right to allocate Tensor Memory.
511	///
512	/// After any thread in a CTA executes this, no further `tcgen05_alloc` calls
513	/// are allowed from that CTA. This is an optional optimization hint.
514	///
515	/// This is a **warp-synchronous** operation.
516	///
517	/// # PTX
518	///
519	/// ```ptx
520	/// tcgen05.relinquish_alloc_permit.cta_group::1.sync.aligned;
521	/// ```
522	#[inline(never)]
523	pub fn tcgen05_relinquish_alloc_permit() {
524	    unreachable!("tcgen05_relinquish_alloc_permit called outside CUDA kernel context")
525	}

527	// =============================================================================
528	// Synchronization Primitives
529	// =============================================================================
530	
531	/// Fence for ordering BEFORE thread synchronization.
532	///
533	/// Ensures all prior tcgen05 async operations complete before any subsequent
534	/// thread synchronization. Use this when signaling to other threads via relaxed
535	/// memory operations.
536	///
537	/// # When to Use
538	///
539	/// ```rust,ignore
540	/// // Producer thread:
541	/// tcgen05_mma_ws_f16(...);
542	/// tcgen05_commit(&mbar);
543	/// tcgen05_fence_before_thread_sync();  // Ensure MMA done before signal
544	/// flag = 1;  // Relaxed store to signal consumer
545	/// ```
546	///
547	/// # Note
548	///
549	/// If you use `mbarrier_try_wait()` for synchronization, you likely DON'T need
550	/// this fence - mbarrier provides the ordering guarantee.
551	///
552	/// # PTX
553	///
554	/// ```ptx
555	/// tcgen05.fence::before_thread_sync;
556	/// ```
557	#[inline(never)]
558	pub fn tcgen05_fence_before_thread_sync() {
559	    unreachable!("tcgen05_fence_before_thread_sync called outside CUDA kernel context")
560	}
561	
562	/// Fence for ordering AFTER thread synchronization.
563	///
564	/// Ensures all subsequent tcgen05 operations wait until after thread
565	/// synchronization completes. Use this when receiving signals from other threads.
566	///
567	/// # When to Use
568	///
569	/// ```rust,ignore
570	/// // Consumer thread:
571	/// while flag == 0 {}  // Relaxed load spin
572	/// tcgen05_fence_after_thread_sync();  // Ensure signal received before TMEM access
573	/// // Now safe to access TMEM results
574	/// ```
575	///
576	/// # Note
577	///
578	/// If you use `mbarrier_try_wait()` for synchronization, you likely DON'T need
579	/// this fence - mbarrier provides the ordering guarantee.
580	///
581	/// # PTX
582	///
583	/// ```ptx
584	/// tcgen05.fence::after_thread_sync;
585	/// ```
586	#[inline(never)]
587	pub fn tcgen05_fence_after_thread_sync() {
588	    unreachable!("tcgen05_fence_after_thread_sync called outside CUDA kernel context")
589	}
590	
591	/// Commit pending tcgen05 operations to an mbarrier.
592	///
593	/// This signals the mbarrier when all prior tcgen05 async operations complete.
594	/// Use `mbarrier_try_wait()` to wait for completion.
595	///
596	/// This is a **single-thread** operation - only one thread needs to call this.
597	///
598	/// # Parameters
599	///
600	/// - `mbar`: Pointer to mbarrier object in shared memory
601	///
602	/// # Safety
603	///
604	/// - `mbar` must point to a valid, initialized mbarrier
605	///
606	/// # PTX
607	///
608	/// ```ptx
609	/// tcgen05.commit.cta_group::1.mbarrier::arrive::one.b64 [mbar];
610	/// ```
611	#[inline(never)]
612	pub unsafe fn tcgen05_commit(mbar: *mut u64) {
613	    let _ = mbar;
614	    unreachable!("tcgen05_commit called outside CUDA kernel context")
615	}
616	
617	/// Commit tcgen05 work and arrive at the barrier using the `.shared::cluster` variant.
618	///
619	/// # Safety
620	///
621	/// - `mbar` must be a valid pointer to a barrier in shared memory
622	/// - Must be called from within a CUDA kernel context on sm_100a+
623	///
624	/// PTX:
625	/// ```ptx
626	/// tcgen05.commit.cta_group::1.mbarrier::arrive::one.shared::cluster.b64 [mbar];
627	/// ```
628	#[inline(never)]
629	pub unsafe fn tcgen05_commit_shared_cluster(mbar: *mut u64) {
630	    let _ = mbar;
631	    unreachable!("tcgen05_commit_shared_cluster called outside CUDA kernel context")
632	}

704	/// Instruction descriptor for tcgen05 MMA operations.
705	///
706	/// The 32-bit descriptor encodes the matrix shapes, data types, sparsity mode,
707	/// and other operation details according to the PTX ISA specification.
708	///
709	/// # Bit Layout for .kind::tf32, .kind::f16 (from PTX ISA 8.6+)
710	///
711	/// ```text
712	/// Bits  | Field              | Description
713	/// ------+--------------------+------------------------------------------
714	/// 0-1   | Sparsity selector  | 0-3 (if sparsity enabled)
715	/// 2     | Sparsity           | 0 = Dense, 1 = Sparse
716	/// 3     | Reserved           | 0
717	/// 4-5   | dtype              | D type: F16=0, F32=1
718	/// 6     | Reserved           | 0
719	/// 7-9   | atype              | A type: F16=0, BF16=1, TF32=2
720	/// 10-12 | btype              | B type: F16=0, BF16=1, TF32=2
721	/// 13    | Negate A           | 0 = No, 1 = Yes
722	/// 14    | Negate B           | 0 = No, 1 = Yes
723	/// 15    | Transpose A        | 0 = No, 1 = Yes
724	/// 16    | Transpose B        | 0 = No, 1 = Yes
725	/// 17-22 | N dimension        | N >> 3 (N/8)
726	/// 23    | Reserved           | 0
727	/// 24-28 | M dimension        | M >> 4 (M/16)
728	/// 29    | Reserved           | 0
729	/// 30-31 | B reuse shift      | 0=none, 1=8, 2=16, 3=32
730	/// ```
731	///
732	/// # Example
733	///
734	/// ```rust,ignore
735	/// // Create descriptor for 128×256 f16 MMA with f32 accumulator
736	/// let idesc = Tcgen05InstructionDescriptor::builder()
737	///     .shape(Tcgen05MmaShape::M128_N256)
738	///     .element_type(Tcgen05ElementType::F16)
739	///     .accumulator_type(Tcgen05AccumulatorType::F32)
740	///     .build();
741	/// ```
742	#[repr(transparent)]
743	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
744	pub struct Tcgen05InstructionDescriptor {
745	    raw: u32,
746	}
747	
748	impl Tcgen05InstructionDescriptor {
749	    /// Create a new instruction descriptor builder.
750	    #[inline(always)]
751	    pub const fn builder() -> Tcgen05InstructionDescriptorBuilder {
752	        Tcgen05InstructionDescriptorBuilder::new()
753	    }
754	
755	    /// Create descriptor for f16 MMA with default settings.
756	    ///
757	    /// Default: 128×256 shape, F16 inputs, F32 accumulator, dense, no transpose.
758	    #[inline(always)]
759	    pub const fn new_f16() -> Self {
760	        Self::builder()
761	            .shape(Tcgen05MmaShape::M128_N256)
762	            .element_type(Tcgen05ElementType::F16)
763	            .accumulator_type(Tcgen05AccumulatorType::F32)
764	            .build()
765	    }
766	
767	    /// Create descriptor for bf16 MMA with default settings.
768	    ///
769	    /// Default: 128×256 shape, BF16 inputs, F32 accumulator, dense, no transpose.
770	    #[inline(always)]
771	    pub const fn new_bf16() -> Self {
772	        Self::builder()
773	            .shape(Tcgen05MmaShape::M128_N256)
774	            .element_type(Tcgen05ElementType::BF16)
775	            .accumulator_type(Tcgen05AccumulatorType::F32)
776	            .build()
777	    }
778	
779	    /// Create descriptor for tf32 MMA with default settings.
780	    ///
781	    /// Default: 128×256 shape, TF32 inputs, F32 accumulator, dense, no transpose.
782	    #[inline(always)]
783	    pub const fn new_tf32() -> Self {
784	        Self::builder()
785	            .shape(Tcgen05MmaShape::M128_N256)
786	            .element_type(Tcgen05ElementType::TF32)
787	            .accumulator_type(Tcgen05AccumulatorType::F32)
788	            .build()
789	    }
790	
791	    /// Create descriptor from raw 32-bit value.
792	    #[inline(always)]
793	    pub const fn from_raw(raw: u32) -> Self {
794	        Self { raw }
795	    }
796	
797	    /// Get the raw 32-bit descriptor value.
798	    #[inline(always)]
799	    pub const fn raw(self) -> u32 {
800	        self.raw
801	    }
802	}

804	/// Builder for `Tcgen05InstructionDescriptor`.
805	///
806	/// Allows fluent construction of instruction descriptors with compile-time
807	/// validation of common configurations.
808	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
809	pub struct Tcgen05InstructionDescriptorBuilder {
810	    // Bit 2: Sparsity (0 = dense)
811	    sparse: bool,
812	    // Bits 4-5: dtype
813	    dtype: Tcgen05AccumulatorType,
814	    // Bits 7-9: atype (and btype, typically same)
815	    atype: Tcgen05ElementType,
816	    // Bits 10-12: btype
817	    btype: Tcgen05ElementType,
818	    // Bit 13: negate A
819	    negate_a: bool,
820	    // Bit 14: negate B
821	    negate_b: bool,
822	    // Bit 15: transpose A
823	    transpose_a: bool,
824	    // Bit 16: transpose B
825	    transpose_b: bool,
826	    // Bits 17-22: N >> 3
827	    n_dim: u32,
828	    // Bits 24-28: M >> 4
829	    m_dim: u32,
830	    // Bits 30-31: B reuse shift
831	    b_reuse_shift: u8,
832	}

834	impl Tcgen05InstructionDescriptorBuilder {
835	    /// Create a new builder with default settings.

837	    /// Defaults:

846	            sparse: false,

850	            negate_a: false,
851	            negate_b: false,
852	            transpose_a: false,
853	            transpose_b: false,
854	            n_dim: 256,
855	            m_dim: 128,
856	            b_reuse_shift: 0,

893	        self.atype = ty;
894	        self.btype = ty;
895	        self
896	    }
897	
898	    /// Set element type for matrix A only.
899	    #[inline(always)]
900	    pub const fn a_type(mut self, ty: Tcgen05ElementType) -> Self {
901	        self.atype = ty;
902	        self
903	    }
904	
905	    /// Set element type for matrix B only.
906	    #[inline(always)]
907	    pub const fn b_type(mut self, ty: Tcgen05ElementType) -> Self {
908	        self.btype = ty;
909	        self
910	    }

915	        self.dtype = ty;
916	        self
917	    }
918	
919	    /// Enable sparse matrix A.
920	    ///
921	    /// When enabled, K dimension is doubled (K=32 for f16, K=16 for tf32).
922	    #[inline(always)]
923	    pub const fn sparse(mut self, enable: bool) -> Self {
924	        self.sparse = enable;
925	        self
926	    }
927	
928	    /// Enable negation of matrix A values.
929	    #[inline(always)]
930	    pub const fn negate_a(mut self, enable: bool) -> Self {
931	        self.negate_a = enable;
932	        self
933	    }
934	
935	    /// Enable negation of matrix B values.
936	    #[inline(always)]
937	    pub const fn negate_b(mut self, enable: bool) -> Self {
938	        self.negate_b = enable;
939	        self
940	    }
941	
942	    /// Enable transposition of matrix A.
943	    #[inline(always)]
944	    pub const fn transpose_a(mut self, enable: bool) -> Self {
945	        self.transpose_a = enable;
946	        self
947	    }
948	
949	    /// Enable transposition of matrix B.
950	    #[inline(always)]
951	    pub const fn transpose_b(mut self, enable: bool) -> Self {
952	        self.transpose_b = enable;
953	        self
954	    }
955	
956	    /// Set maximum B matrix reuse shift.
957	    ///
958	    /// - 0: No shift (default)
959	    /// - 1: Maximum shift of 8
960	    /// - 2: Maximum shift of 16
961	    /// - 3: Maximum shift of 32
962	    #[inline(always)]
963	    pub const fn b_reuse_shift(mut self, shift: u8) -> Self {
964	        self.b_reuse_shift = shift;
965	        self
966	    }
967	
968	    /// Build the instruction descriptor.
969	    ///
970	    /// Encodes all settings into the 32-bit descriptor format.
971	    #[inline(always)]
972	    pub const fn build(self) -> Tcgen05InstructionDescriptor {
973	        let mut raw: u32 = 0;
974	
975	        // Bits 0-1: Sparsity selector (always 0 for now)
976	        // raw |= 0;
977	
978	        // Bit 2: Sparsity
979	        if self.sparse {
980	            raw |= 1 << 2;
981	        }
982	
983	        // Bit 3: Reserved (0)
984	
985	        // Bits 4-5: dtype (accumulator type)
986	        raw |= (self.dtype as u32) << 4;
987	
988	        // Bit 6: Reserved (0)
989	
990	        // Bits 7-9: atype (3 bits)
991	        raw |= (self.atype as u32) << 7;
992	
993	        // Bits 10-12: btype (3 bits)
994	        raw |= (self.btype as u32) << 10;
995	
996	        // Bit 13: Negate A
997	        if self.negate_a {
998	            raw |= 1 << 13;
999	        }
1000	
1001	        // Bit 14: Negate B
1002	        if self.negate_b {
1003	            raw |= 1 << 14;
1004	        }
1005	
1006	        // Bit 15: Transpose A
1007	        if self.transpose_a {
1008	            raw |= 1 << 15;
1009	        }
1010	
1011	        // Bit 16: Transpose B
1012	        if self.transpose_b {
1013	            raw |= 1 << 16;
1014	        }
1015	
1016	        // Bits 17-22: N >> 3 (6 bits)
1017	        raw |= ((self.n_dim >> 3) & 0x3F) << 17;
1018	
1019	        // Bit 23: Reserved (0)
1020	
1021	        // Bits 24-28: M >> 4 (5 bits)
1022	        raw |= ((self.m_dim >> 4) & 0x1F) << 24;
1023	
1024	        // Bit 29: Reserved (0)
1025	
1026	        // Bits 30-31: B reuse shift (2 bits)
1027	        raw |= ((self.b_reuse_shift as u32) & 0x3) << 30;
1028	
1029	        Tcgen05InstructionDescriptor { raw }
1030	    }
1031	}
1032	
1033	impl Default for Tcgen05InstructionDescriptorBuilder {
1034	    fn default() -> Self {
1035	        Self::new()
1036	    }
1037	}

1039	// =============================================================================
1040	// SMEM Descriptor
1041	// =============================================================================
1042	
1043	/// Swizzle modes for tcgen05 SMEM descriptors.
1044	///
1045	/// Swizzling reorders data in shared memory to avoid bank conflicts during
1046	/// tensor core operations. The mode determines the XOR pattern applied to
1047	/// addresses.
1048	///
1049	/// # Encoding (bits 61-63 of SMEM descriptor)
1050	///
1051	/// | Mode | Value | Pattern Start |
1052	/// |------|-------|---------------|
1053	/// | None | 0 | - |
1054	/// | 128B + 32B atomicity | 1 | 1024B boundary |
1055	/// | 128B | 2 | 1024B boundary |
1056	/// | 64B | 4 | 512B boundary |
1057	/// | 32B | 6 | 256B boundary |
1058	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
1059	#[repr(u8)]
1060	pub enum Tcgen05SwizzleMode {
1061	    /// No swizzling
1062	    #[default]
1063	    None = 0,
1064	    /// 128-byte swizzle with 32-byte atomicity (best for tensor cores)
1065	    Swizzle128B32BAtom = 1,
1066	    /// 128-byte swizzle (most common for tensor cores)
1067	    Swizzle128B = 2,
1068	    /// 64-byte swizzle
1069	    Swizzle64B = 4,
1070	    /// 32-byte swizzle
1071	    Swizzle32B = 6,
1072	}
1073	
1074	/// SMEM descriptor for tcgen05 MMA operations (64-bit).
1075	///
1076	/// Describes a matrix tile in shared memory for tcgen05.mma instructions.
1077	/// The descriptor encodes the start address, stride dimensions, and swizzle mode.
1078	///
1079	/// # Bit Layout (from PTX ISA)
1080	///
1081	/// ```text
1082	/// Bits  | Field
1083	/// ------+----------------------------------------------------------
1084	/// 0-13  | (start_address >> 4) & 0x3FFF
1085	/// 14-15 | Reserved
1086	/// 16-29 | (leading_dim_bytes >> 4) & 0x3FFF
1087	/// 30-31 | Reserved
1088	/// 32-45 | (stride_bytes >> 4) & 0x3FFF
1089	/// 46-48 | Fixed: 0b001
1090	/// 49-51 | Base offset (for non-aligned swizzle patterns)
1091	/// 52    | Leading dim mode: 0=relative, 1=absolute
1092	/// 53-60 | Fixed: 0x00
1093	/// 61-63 | Swizzle mode
1094	/// ```
1095	///
1096	/// # Example
1097	///
1098	/// ```rust,ignore
1099	/// // For 128×16 f16 matrix A in K-major tiled layout:
1100	/// let desc = Tcgen05SmemDescriptor::for_k_major(
1101	///     smem_a_ptr as u64,
1102	///     128,  // M dimension
1103	///     16,   // K dimension
1104	///     2,    // f16 = 2 bytes per element
1105	///     Tcgen05SwizzleMode::None,
1106	/// );
1107	///
1108	/// // Use in MMA
1109	/// tcgen05_mma_f16(d_tmem, desc.raw(), b_desc, idesc, true);
1110	/// ```
1111	#[repr(transparent)]
1112	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
1113	pub struct Tcgen05SmemDescriptor {
1114	    raw: u64,
1115	}
1116	
1117	impl Tcgen05SmemDescriptor {
1118	    /// Create a new SMEM descriptor builder.
1119	    #[inline(always)]
1120	    pub const fn builder() -> Tcgen05SmemDescriptorBuilder {
1121	        Tcgen05SmemDescriptorBuilder::new()
1122	    }
1123	
1124	    /// Build descriptor for K-major tiled layout (matrix A).
1125	    ///
1126	    /// K-major layout is required for the A matrix in tcgen05.mma:
1127	    /// - Matrix divided into 8×8 tiles
1128	    /// - Tiles arranged column-major
1129	    /// - Within each tile: row-major (K dimension contiguous)
1130	    ///
1131	    /// # Parameters
1132	    ///
1133	    /// - `smem_addr`: Shared memory address (must be 16-byte aligned)
1134	    /// - `m`: M dimension (rows of the matrix)
1135	    /// - `k`: K dimension (columns of the matrix)
1136	    /// - `elem_bytes`: Element size in bytes (2 for f16/bf16, 4 for tf32/f32)
1137	    /// - `swizzle`: Swizzle mode for bank conflict avoidance
1138	    ///
1139	    /// # Layout Computation
1140	    ///
1141	    /// For an M×K matrix with 8×8 tiles:
1142	    /// - **SBO** (stride byte offset) = 8 × 8 × elem_bytes = 64 × elem_bytes
1143	    /// - **LBO** (leading dim byte offset) = (M/8) × 8 × 8 × elem_bytes
1144	    ///
1145	    /// # Example
1146	    ///
1147	    /// ```rust,ignore
1148	    /// // 128×16 f16 matrix
1149	    /// let desc = Tcgen05SmemDescriptor::for_k_major(
1150	    ///     smem_ptr as u64, 128, 16, 2, Tcgen05SwizzleMode::None
1151	    /// );
1152	    /// // SBO = 8*8*2 = 128 bytes
1153	    /// // LBO = (128/8)*8*8*2 = 16*64*2 = 2048 bytes
1154	    /// ```
1155	    #[inline(always)]
1156	    pub const fn for_k_major(
1157	        smem_addr: u64,
1158	        m: usize,
1159	        k: usize,
1160	        elem_bytes: usize,
1161	        swizzle: Tcgen05SwizzleMode,
1162	    ) -> Self {
1163	        let _ = k; // K determines number of tiles horizontally, but not SBO/LBO directly
1164	
1165	        // SBO = one 8×8 tile size in bytes
1166	        let sbo = (8 * 8 * elem_bytes) as u32;
1167	
1168	        // LBO = column of tiles = (M/8) tiles × 64 elements × elem_bytes
1169	        let lbo = ((m / 8) * 8 * 8 * elem_bytes) as u32;
1170	
1171	        Self::builder()
1172	            .address(smem_addr)
1173	            .leading_dim_bytes(lbo)
1174	            .stride_bytes(sbo)
1175	            .swizzle(swizzle)
1176	            .build()
1177	    }
1178	
1179	    /// Build descriptor for MN-major tiled layout (matrix B).
1180	    ///
1181	    /// MN-major layout is required for the B matrix in tcgen05.mma:
1182	    /// - Matrix divided into 8×8 tiles
1183	    /// - Tiles arranged column-major
1184	    /// - Within each tile: column-major (MN dimension contiguous)
1185	    ///
1186	    /// # Parameters
1187	    ///
1188	    /// - `smem_addr`: Shared memory address (must be 16-byte aligned)
1189	    /// - `n`: N dimension (rows of B, which is K×N transposed view)
1190	    /// - `k`: K dimension
1191	    /// - `elem_bytes`: Element size in bytes
1192	    /// - `swizzle`: Swizzle mode
1193	    #[inline(always)]
1194	    pub const fn for_mn_major(
1195	        smem_addr: u64,
1196	        n: usize,
1197	        k: usize,
1198	        elem_bytes: usize,
1199	        swizzle: Tcgen05SwizzleMode,
1200	    ) -> Self {
1201	        let _ = k;
1202	
1203	        let sbo = (8 * 8 * elem_bytes) as u32;
1204	        let lbo = ((n / 8) * 8 * 8 * elem_bytes) as u32;
1205	
1206	        Self::builder()
1207	            .address(smem_addr)
1208	            .leading_dim_bytes(lbo)
1209	            .stride_bytes(sbo)
1210	            .swizzle(swizzle)
1211	            .build()
1212	    }
1213	
1214	    /// Build descriptor from explicit byte offsets.
1215	    ///
1216	    /// Use this when you need full control over the descriptor parameters.
1217	    ///
1218	    /// # Parameters
1219	    ///
1220	    /// - `smem_addr`: Shared memory address (16-byte aligned)
1221	    /// - `leading_dim_bytes`: Leading dimension stride in bytes (LBO)
1222	    /// - `stride_bytes`: Stride dimension offset in bytes (SBO)
1223	    /// - `swizzle`: Swizzle mode
1224	    #[inline(always)]
1225	    pub const fn from_bytes(
1226	        smem_addr: u64,
1227	        leading_dim_bytes: u32,
1228	        stride_bytes: u32,
1229	        swizzle: Tcgen05SwizzleMode,
1230	    ) -> Self {
1231	        Self::builder()
1232	            .address(smem_addr)
1233	            .leading_dim_bytes(leading_dim_bytes)
1234	            .stride_bytes(stride_bytes)
1235	            .swizzle(swizzle)
1236	            .build()
1237	    }
1238	
1239	    /// Create descriptor from raw 64-bit value.
1240	    #[inline(always)]
1241	    pub const fn from_raw(raw: u64) -> Self {
1242	        Self { raw }
1243	    }
1244	
1245	    /// Get the raw 64-bit descriptor value.
1246	    #[inline(always)]
1247	    pub const fn raw(self) -> u64 {
1248	        self.raw
1249	    }
1250	}

1252	/// Builder for `Tcgen05SmemDescriptor`.
1253	///
1254	/// Provides fluent API for constructing SMEM descriptors with compile-time
1255	/// validation of common configurations.
1256	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
1257	pub struct Tcgen05SmemDescriptorBuilder {
1258	    address: u64,
1259	    leading_dim_bytes: u32,
1260	    stride_bytes: u32,
1261	    base_offset: u8,
1262	    leading_dim_absolute: bool,
1263	    swizzle: Tcgen05SwizzleMode,
1264	}
1265	
1266	impl Tcgen05SmemDescriptorBuilder {
1267	    /// Create a new builder with default settings.
1268	    #[inline(always)]
1269	    pub const fn new() -> Self {
1270	        Self {
1271	            address: 0,
1272	            leading_dim_bytes: 0,
1273	            stride_bytes: 0,
1274	            base_offset: 0,
1275	            leading_dim_absolute: false,
1276	            swizzle: Tcgen05SwizzleMode::None,
1277	        }
1278	    }
1279	
1280	    /// Set the shared memory start address.
1281	    ///
1282	    /// Must be 16-byte aligned.
1283	    #[inline(always)]
1284	    pub const fn address(mut self, addr: u64) -> Self {
1285	        self.address = addr;
1286	        self
1287	    }
1288	
1289	    /// Set the leading dimension byte offset (LBO).
1290	    ///
1291	    /// This is the stride to move to the next "column" of tiles.
1292	    /// Must be 16-byte aligned.
1293	    #[inline(always)]
1294	    pub const fn leading_dim_bytes(mut self, lbo: u32) -> Self {
1295	        self.leading_dim_bytes = lbo;
1296	        self
1297	    }
1298	
1299	    /// Set the stride dimension byte offset (SBO).
1300	    ///
1301	    /// This is typically the size of one tile (8×8 × element_size).
1302	    /// Must be 16-byte aligned.
1303	    #[inline(always)]
1304	    pub const fn stride_bytes(mut self, sbo: u32) -> Self {
1305	        self.stride_bytes = sbo;
1306	        self
1307	    }
1308	
1309	    /// Set the swizzle mode.
1310	    #[inline(always)]
1311	    pub const fn swizzle(mut self, mode: Tcgen05SwizzleMode) -> Self {
1312	        self.swizzle = mode;
1313	        self
1314	    }
1315	
1316	    /// Set the base offset for non-aligned swizzle patterns.
1317	    ///
1318	    /// Only needed when the swizzle pattern doesn't start at a natural boundary.
1319	    /// Formula: `base_offset = (pattern_start_addr >> 7) & 0x7`
1320	    #[inline(always)]
1321	    pub const fn base_offset(mut self, offset: u8) -> Self {
1322	        self.base_offset = offset;
1323	        self
1324	    }
1325	
1326	    /// Use absolute addressing for leading dimension.
1327	    ///
1328	    /// When true, leading_dim_bytes is an absolute address rather than
1329	    /// a relative offset. Only supported on sm_103a+.
1330	    #[inline(always)]
1331	    pub const fn leading_dim_absolute(mut self, absolute: bool) -> Self {
1332	        self.leading_dim_absolute = absolute;
1333	        self
1334	    }
1335	
1336	    /// Build the SMEM descriptor.
1337	    ///
1338	    /// Encodes all settings into the 64-bit descriptor format.
1339	    #[inline(always)]
1340	    pub const fn build(self) -> Tcgen05SmemDescriptor {
1341	        let mut raw: u64 = 0;
1342	
1343	        // Bits 0-13: (address >> 4) & 0x3FFF
1344	        raw |= (self.address >> 4) & 0x3FFF;
1345	
1346	        // Bits 16-29: (leading_dim_bytes >> 4) & 0x3FFF
1347	        raw |= (((self.leading_dim_bytes >> 4) & 0x3FFF) as u64) << 16;
1348	
1349	        // Bits 32-45: (stride_bytes >> 4) & 0x3FFF
1350	        raw |= (((self.stride_bytes >> 4) & 0x3FFF) as u64) << 32;
1351	
1352	        // Bits 46-48: Fixed = 0b001
1353	        raw |= 1u64 << 46;
1354	
1355	        // Bits 49-51: Base offset (3 bits)
1356	        raw |= ((self.base_offset & 0x7) as u64) << 49;
1357	
1358	        // Bit 52: Leading dim mode (0 = relative, 1 = absolute)
1359	        if self.leading_dim_absolute {
1360	            raw |= 1u64 << 52;
1361	        }
1362	
1363	        // Bits 53-60: Fixed = 0 (already zero)
1364	
1365	        // Bits 61-63: Swizzle mode (3 bits)
1366	        raw |= (self.swizzle as u64) << 61;
1367	
1368	        Tcgen05SmemDescriptor { raw }
1369	    }
1370	}
1371	
1372	impl Default for Tcgen05SmemDescriptorBuilder {
1373	    fn default() -> Self {
1374	        Self::new()
1375	    }
1376	}

1378	// =============================================================================
1379	// Collector Buffer Usage
1380	// =============================================================================
1381	
1382	/// Collector buffer usage hints for matrix B caching.
1383	///
1384	/// Collector buffers are internal to the Tensor Core (not addressable).
1385	/// They cache matrix B data to avoid repeated shared memory reads when
1386	/// using the same B matrix with different A matrices.
1387	///
1388	/// # Example
1389	///
1390	/// ```rust,ignore
1391	/// // First MMA: Load B into collector b0
1392	/// tcgen05_mma_ws_f16_with_collector(..., CollectorUsage::Fill(0));
1393	///
1394	/// // Subsequent MMAs: Reuse B from collector
1395	/// tcgen05_mma_ws_f16_with_collector(..., CollectorUsage::Use(0));
1396	/// tcgen05_mma_ws_f16_with_collector(..., CollectorUsage::Use(0));
1397	///
1398	/// // Last use: Discard after using
1399	/// tcgen05_mma_ws_f16_with_collector(..., CollectorUsage::LastUse(0));
1400	/// ```
1401	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
1402	pub enum CollectorUsage {
1403	    /// Read B from SMEM and cache in buffer N
1404	    Fill(u8),
1405	    /// Use B from buffer N (must have been filled)
1406	    Use(u8),
1407	    /// Use B from buffer N, then discard
1408	    LastUse(u8),
1409	    /// Discard buffer N without using
1410	    Discard(u8),
1411	}

1417	/// tcgen05 Matrix Multiply-Accumulate with f16 inputs.
1418	///
1419	/// Performs D = A × B + D (or D = A × B if `enable_d` is false).
1420	///
1421	/// **SINGLE-THREAD SEMANTICS**: Unlike WGMMA which requires 128 threads,
1422	/// ONE thread issues the entire MMA operation!
1423	///
1424	/// # Matrix Convention
1425	///
1426	/// - **A**: Left multiplicand (M×K) in Tensor Memory
1427	/// - **B**: Right multiplicand (K×N) in Shared Memory
1428	/// - **D**: Destination AND accumulator (M×N) in Tensor Memory
1429	///
1430	/// # Parameters
1431	///
1432	/// - `d_tmem`: TMEM address for D matrix (destination/accumulator)
1433	/// - `a_tmem`: TMEM address for A matrix

1435	/// - `b_desc`: SMEM descriptor for B (from `Tcgen05SmemDescriptor::builder()`)
1436	/// - `idesc`: Instruction descriptor (from `Tcgen05InstructionDescriptor`)
1437	/// - `enable_d`: true = D += A×B, false = D = A×B
1438	///
1439	/// # Safety
1440	///
1441	/// - TMEM addresses must have been allocated with `tcgen05_alloc`
1442	/// - Descriptors must point to valid shared memory
1443	///
1444	/// # PTX
1445	///
1446	/// ```ptx
1447	/// tcgen05.mma.ws.cta_group::1.kind::f16 [d-tmem], [a-tmem], a-desc, b-desc, idesc, enable-d;
1448	/// ```
1449	#[inline(never)]
1450	pub unsafe fn tcgen05_mma_ws_f16(
1451	    d_tmem: u32,
1452	    a_tmem: u32,
1453	    a_desc: u64,
1454	    b_desc: u64,
1455	    idesc: u32,
1456	    enable_d: bool,
1457	) {
1458	    let _ = (d_tmem, a_tmem, a_desc, b_desc, idesc, enable_d);
1459	    unreachable!("tcgen05_mma_ws_f16 called outside CUDA kernel context")
1460	}

1589	// NOTE: The following deprecated intrinsics were removed:
1590	// - tcgen05_st_tmem_to_smem (wrong approach - no direct TMEM→SMEM instruction)
1591	// - tcgen05_st_tmem_to_smem_offset (wrong approach)
1592	// - tcgen05_ld_16x256b_x4/x8/x16/x32 (wrong - stored to SMEM instead of returning registers)
1593	// - tcgen05_ld_32x32b_x64 (wrong - stored to SMEM instead of returning registers)
1594	//
1595	// Use tcgen05_ld_16x256b_pure or tcgen05_ld_16x256b_x8_pure instead.
1596	// These return values in registers, allowing proper epilogue processing:
1597	//   1. Load from TMEM → registers
1598	//   2. Convert f32 → bf16/f16 in registers
1599	//   3. Store to SMEM via stmatrix
1600	
1601	// =============================================================================
1602	// Pure TMEM Load (Returns Registers - No SMEM Store)
1603	// =============================================================================
1604	
1605	use crate::cusimd::CuSimd;
1606	
1607	/// Result of `tcgen05_ld_16x256b_x8_pure` - 32 f32 values per thread.
1608	///
1609	/// This is a type alias for `CuSimd<f32, 32>` which provides:
1610	/// - Array-style indexing: `regs[i]` for runtime access
1611	/// - Const generic access: `regs.get::<0>()` for compile-time access
1612	/// - Shorthand accessors: `regs.x()`, `regs.y()`, etc.
1613	///
1614	/// # Usage
1615	///
1616	/// ```rust,ignore
1617	/// let regs: TmemF32x32 = tcgen05_ld_16x256b_x8_pure(tmem_addr);
1618	/// tcgen05_load_wait();
1619	///
1620	/// // Access via index
1621	/// let val0 = regs[0];
1622	/// let val1 = regs[1];
1623	///
1624	/// // Convert and pack for stmatrix
1625	/// let p0 = cvt_f32x2_bf16x2(regs[0], regs[1]);
1626	/// let p1 = cvt_f32x2_bf16x2(regs[2], regs[3]);
1627	/// // ...
1628	/// stmatrix_m8n8_x4_trans(smem_ptr, p0, p1, p2, p3);
1629	/// ```
1630	pub type TmemF32x32 = CuSimd<f32, 32>;
1631	
1632	/// Load 32 f32 values from TMEM to registers (PURE - no SMEM store).
1633	///
1634	/// This is the **correct** way to load from TMEM for epilog processing.
1635	/// Unlike `tcgen05_ld_16x256b_x8`, this function:
1636	/// - Returns values in registers (as a CuSimd)
1637	/// - Does NOT store to shared memory
1638	/// - Allows subsequent in-register operations (convert, pack, stmatrix)
1639	///
1640	/// # Returns
1641	///
1642	/// `TmemF32x32` (`CuSimd<f32, 32>`) containing 32 f32 values per thread.
1643	/// For a warp: 32 threads × 32 values = 1024 f32 = 16 rows × 64 columns.
1644	///
1645	/// # PTX
1646	///
1647	/// ```ptx
1648	/// tcgen05.ld.sync.aligned.16x256b.x8.b32
1649	///   {%r0,%r1,...,%r31}, [%tmem_addr];
1650	/// ```
1651	///
1652	/// # Safety
1653	///
1654	/// - `tmem_addr` must be valid (from tcgen05_alloc)
1655	/// - Must be called by ALL 32 threads in a warp together (warp-synchronous)
1656	/// - Must call `tcgen05_load_wait()` before accessing returned values
1657	#[inline(never)]
1658	pub unsafe fn tcgen05_ld_16x256b_x8_pure(tmem_addr: u32) -> TmemF32x32 {
1659	    let _ = tmem_addr;
1660	    unreachable!("tcgen05_ld_16x256b_x8_pure called outside CUDA kernel context")
1661	}

1663	// =============================================================================
1664	// Base LDTM (16dp256bit without .x multiplier)
1665	// =============================================================================
1666	
1667	/// Result of `tcgen05_ld_16x256b_pure` (base LDTM) - 4 f32 values per thread.
1668	///
1669	/// This is a type alias for `CuSimd<f32, 4>` which provides:
1670	/// - Array-style indexing: `regs[i]` for runtime access
1671	/// - Const generic access: `regs.get::<0>()` for compile-time access
1672	/// - Shorthand accessors: `regs.x()`, `regs.y()`, `regs.z()`, `regs.w()`
1673	///
1674	/// # Thread Data Layout
1675	///
1676	/// For base LDTM.16dp256bit with 32dp (data parallelism = 32 threads):
1677	/// - Each thread receives 4 f32 values = 16 bytes = 128 bits
1678	/// - Total per warp: 32 threads × 4 values = 128 f32 values
1679	/// - This corresponds to an 8×16 matrix tile (8 rows × 16 columns)
1680	///
1681	/// # Relationship to stmatrix.x2
1682	///
1683	/// The data layout from base LDTM matches perfectly with `stmatrix.m8n8.x2`:
1684	/// - 128 bf16 (after conversion) = 2 matrices × 8×8 = 128 elements ✓
1685	/// - Thread ownership aligns: each thread owns data for specific positions
1686	///
1687	/// # Usage
1688	///
1689	/// ```rust,ignore

1745	/// Convert two f32 values to packed bf16x2 using PTX.
1746	///
1747	/// This is the **proper** way to convert f32 → bf16 for stmatrix.
1748	/// Uses the PTX `cvt.rn.bf16x2.f32` instruction which:
1749	/// - Converts with round-to-nearest-even
1750	/// - Packs two bf16 into one u32 register
1751	///
1752	/// # PTX
1753	///
1754	/// ```ptx
1755	/// cvt.rn.bf16x2.f32 %result, %b, %a;
1756	/// // Result: lower 16 bits = bf16(a), upper 16 bits = bf16(b)
1757	/// ```
1758	///
1759	/// # Arguments
1760	///
1761	/// - `a`: First f32 value (goes to lower 16 bits)
1762	/// - `b`: Second f32 value (goes to upper 16 bits)
1763	///
1764	/// # Returns
1765	///
1766	/// Packed u32 with two bf16 values: `(bf16(b) << 16) | bf16(a)`
1767	///
1768	/// Lane placement: the first argument (`a`) fills bits `[15:0]` and the
1769	/// second argument (`b`) fills bits `[31:16]`, even though the PTX
1770	/// operand list prints `b` first. This is the same first-arg-low
1771	/// convention as [`cvt_f16x2_f32`](crate::convert::cvt_f16x2_f32),
1772	/// which differs only in its destination element type (f16, not bf16)
1773	/// and its `lo`/`hi` argument naming.
1774	///
1775	/// # Example
1776	///
1777	/// ```rust,ignore
1778	/// let regs = tcgen05_ld_16x256b_x8_pure(addr);
1779	/// tcgen05_load_wait();
1780	///
1781	/// // Convert pairs of f32 to packed bf16
1782	/// let p0 = cvt_f32x2_bf16x2(regs[0], regs[1]);
1783	/// let p1 = cvt_f32x2_bf16x2(regs[2], regs[3]);
1784	/// let p2 = cvt_f32x2_bf16x2(regs[4], regs[5]);
1785	/// let p3 = cvt_f32x2_bf16x2(regs[6], regs[7]);
1786	///
1787	/// // Store via stmatrix
1788	/// stmatrix_m8n8_x4_trans(smem_ptr, p0, p1, p2, p3);
1789	/// ```
1790	#[inline(never)]
1791	pub fn cvt_f32x2_bf16x2(a: f32, b: f32) -> u32 {
1792	    let _ = (a, b);
1793	    unreachable!("cvt_f32x2_bf16x2 called outside CUDA kernel context")
1794	}

1817	/// // Now safe to read from smem_ptr

1821	///
1822	/// - Must be called by ALL 32 threads in a warp together (warp-synchronous)

1827	/// tcgen05.wait::ld.sync.aligned;
1828	/// ```

1831	    unreachable!("tcgen05_load_wait called outside CUDA kernel context")

1852	/// Store matrix tile from registers to shared memory using stmatrix.
1853	///
1854	/// This is the warp-cooperative matrix store instruction that properly handles
1855	/// the matrix layout when storing MMA results to shared memory.
1856	///
1857	/// # Parameters
1858	///
1859	/// - `smem_ptr`: Destination in shared memory (16-byte aligned)
1860	/// - `r0`, `r1`, `r2`, `r3`: Four u32 values, each containing two packed b16 elements
1861	///
1862	/// # Shape
1863	///
1864	/// `stmatrix.sync.aligned.m8n8.x4.shared.b16`:
1865	/// - Stores four 8×8 matrix tiles
1866	/// - Uses 4 registers per thread (packed as 16-bit elements)
1867	/// - Cooperatively distributes across 32 threads
1868	///
1869	/// # Usage
1870	///
1871	/// Convert loaded values to packed b16 pairs before calling this function.
1872	/// Each u32 is treated as bits; this function does not convert f32 to b16.
1873	///
1874	/// # Address lanes
1875	///
1876	/// ```text
1877	/// lanes  0..7  -> rows 0..7 of matrix 0
1878	/// lanes  8..15 -> rows 0..7 of matrix 1
1879	/// lanes 16..23 -> rows 0..7 of matrix 2
1880	/// lanes 24..31 -> rows 0..7 of matrix 3
1881	/// ```
1882	///
1883	/// # Safety
1884	///
1885	/// - `smem_ptr` must be valid shared memory (16-byte aligned)
1886	/// - Must be called by ALL 32 threads in a warp together
1887	/// - Callers must use the appropriate fence or barrier before a dependent memory access
1888	#[inline(never)]
1889	pub unsafe fn stmatrix_m8n8_x4(smem_ptr: *mut u8, r0: u32, r1: u32, r2: u32, r3: u32) {
1890	    let _ = (smem_ptr, r0, r1, r2, r3);
1891	    unreachable!("stmatrix_m8n8_x4 called outside CUDA kernel context")
1892	}

1894	/// Store matrix tiles from registers to shared memory in column-major order.
1895	///
1896	/// This is the warp-cooperative matrix store instruction with the critical `.trans`
1897	/// modifier, which selects column-major storage.
1898	///
1899	/// # PTX Instruction
1900	///
1901	/// `stmatrix.sync.aligned.m8n8.x4.trans.shared.b16 [ptr], {r0, r1, r2, r3};`
1902	///
1903	/// # Parameters
1904	///
1905	/// - `smem_ptr`: Destination in shared memory (16-byte aligned)
1906	/// - `r0`, `r1`, `r2`, `r3`: Four u32 values, each containing 2 packed bf16 elements
1907	///
1908	/// # Shape
1909	///
1910	/// - Stores four 8×8 matrix tiles (256 elements total per warp call)
1911	/// - Each register contains 2 packed bf16 values
1912	/// - `.trans` stores each matrix in column-major order
1913	///
1914	/// # Usage for 64×64 extraction
1915	///
1916	/// After `tcgen05_ld_16x256b_x8_pure` (32 f32 registers per thread):
1917	/// 1. Convert f32 → bf16 (cvt.rn.bf16.f32)
1918	/// 2. Pack bf16 pairs → u32 (mov.b32)
1919	/// 3. Call `stmatrix_m8n8_x4_trans` 4 times with groups of 4 registers
1920	///
1921	/// Each warp stores 16 rows (1024 elements), so 4 calls × 256 = 1024 ✓
1922	///
1923	/// # Address lanes
1924	///
1925	/// The address-lane rule is unchanged by `.trans`: consecutive groups of
1926	/// eight lanes provide rows 0-7 for matrices 0, 1, 2, and 3 respectively.
1927	///
1928	/// # Safety
1929	///
1930	/// - `smem_ptr` must be valid shared memory (16-byte aligned)
1931	/// - Must be called by ALL 32 threads in a warp together (warp-synchronous)
1932	/// - Registers must contain properly packed bf16 pairs
1933	/// - Callers must use the appropriate fence or barrier before a dependent memory access
1934	#[inline(never)]
1935	pub unsafe fn stmatrix_m8n8_x4_trans(smem_ptr: *mut u8, r0: u32, r1: u32, r2: u32, r3: u32) {
1936	    let _ = (smem_ptr, r0, r1, r2, r3);
1937	    unreachable!("stmatrix_m8n8_x4_trans called outside CUDA kernel context")
1938	}

1940	/// Store 2 matrix tiles (8×8 each = 16 columns) from registers to shared memory.
1941	///
1942	/// This is the warp-cooperative matrix store instruction (NON-transpose version).
1943	/// It stores **16 columns** (2 × 8×8 tiles) per call WITHOUT transpose.
1944	///
1945	/// # PTX Instruction
1946	///
1947	/// `stmatrix.sync.aligned.m8n8.x2.shared.b16 [ptr], {r0, r1};`
1948	///
1949	/// # SASS Instruction
1950	///
1951	/// `STSM.16.MT88.2 [Rx], Ry;`
1952	///
1953	/// # Parameters
1954	///
1955	/// - `smem_ptr`: Base address in shared memory (16-byte aligned)
1956	/// - `r0`, `r1`: Two u32 values, each containing 2 packed bf16 elements
1957	///
1958	/// # Shape
1959	///
1960	/// - Stores two 8×8 matrix tiles (128 bf16 elements total per warp call)
1961	/// - Each thread provides 2 registers = 4 bf16 values
1962	/// - 32 threads × 4 bf16 = 128 bf16 ✓
1963	///
1964	/// # Thread-to-Position Mapping (Non-Trans)
1965	///
1966	/// Without `.trans`, each group of 4 threads stores one row:
1967	/// ```text
1968	/// Row-Major Output (8 rows × 16 columns):
1969	/// ┌────────────────────────────────────┐
1970	/// │ row 0: Threads 0-3's data         │
1971	/// │ row 1: Threads 4-7's data         │
1972	/// │ row 2: Threads 8-11's data        │
1973	/// │ ...                                │
1974	/// │ row 7: Threads 28-31's data       │
1975	/// └────────────────────────────────────┘
1976	/// ```
1977	///
1978	/// Per thread within a row (4 threads per row):
1979	/// - Thread (lane % 4) == 0: columns 0-1 of matrix 0, columns 0-1 of matrix 1
1980	/// - Thread (lane % 4) == 1: columns 2-3 of matrix 0, columns 2-3 of matrix 1
1981	/// - Thread (lane % 4) == 2: columns 4-5 of matrix 0, columns 4-5 of matrix 1
1982	/// - Thread (lane % 4) == 3: columns 6-7 of matrix 0, columns 6-7 of matrix 1
1983	///
1984	/// # Address Requirement
1985	///
1986	/// Only lanes 0-15 provide addresses. Lanes 0-7 provide rows 0-7 of the first
1987	/// matrix; lanes 8-15 provide rows 0-7 of the second matrix. All 32 lanes still
1988	/// execute the instruction and provide fragment registers.
1989	///
1990	/// ```text
1991	/// lanes  0..7  -> base + (lane_id % 8) * row_stride
1992	/// lanes  8..15 -> base + matrix_stride + (lane_id % 8) * row_stride
1993	/// lanes 16..31 -> address operand ignored; fragment registers still used
1994	/// ```
1995	///
1996	/// # Usage with Base LDTM
1997	///
1998	/// ```rust,ignore
1999	/// // Load 4 f32 from TMEM using base LDTM
2000	/// let regs: TmemF32x4 = tcgen05_ld_16x256b_pure(tmem_addr);
2001	/// tcgen05_load_wait();
2002	///
2003	/// // Convert f32 pairs to packed bf16x2
2004	/// let p0 = cvt_f32x2_bf16x2(regs[0], regs[1]);
2005	/// let p1 = cvt_f32x2_bf16x2(regs[2], regs[3]);
2006	///
2007	/// let row_in_tile = lane_id % 8;
2008	/// let matrix_offset = if lane_id < 8 { 0 } else { matrix_stride };
2009	/// let addr = base_smem + matrix_offset + row_in_tile * row_stride + col_offset;
2010	///
2011	/// // Store via stmatrix.x2 (non-trans)
2012	/// stmatrix_m8n8_x2(addr as *mut u8, p0, p1);
2013	/// ```
2014	///
2015	/// # Safety
2016	///
2017	/// - `smem_ptr` must be valid shared memory (16-byte aligned)
2018	/// - Must be called by ALL 32 threads in a warp together (warp-synchronous)
2019	/// - Registers must contain properly packed bf16 pairs
2020	/// - Callers must use the appropriate fence or barrier before a dependent memory access
2021	#[inline(never)]
2022	pub unsafe fn stmatrix_m8n8_x2(smem_ptr: *mut u8, r0: u32, r1: u32) {
2023	    let _ = (smem_ptr, r0, r1);
2024	    unreachable!("stmatrix_m8n8_x2 called outside CUDA kernel context")
2025	}

2031	/// # Generated PTX

2035	/// # Thread-to-Address Mapping (TRANSPOSE)
2036	///
2037	/// The address-lane rule is unchanged by `.trans`: lanes 0-7 provide rows
2038	/// 0-7 of the first matrix and lanes 8-15 provide rows 0-7 of the second.
2039	/// All 32 lanes still execute the instruction and provide fragment registers.
2040	///
2041	/// # Safety
2042	///
2043	/// - `smem_ptr` must be valid shared memory (16-byte aligned)
2044	/// - Must be called by ALL 32 threads in a warp together
2045	/// - Registers must contain properly packed bf16 pairs from LDTM
2046	/// - Callers must use the appropriate fence or barrier before a dependent memory access
2047	#[inline(never)]
2048	pub unsafe fn stmatrix_m8n8_x2_trans(smem_ptr: *mut u8, r0: u32, r1: u32) {
2049	    let _ = (smem_ptr, r0, r1);
2050	    unreachable!("stmatrix_m8n8_x2_trans called outside CUDA kernel context")
2051	}

2053	// =============================================================================
2054	// Type Conversion Helpers (for epilog)
2055	// =============================================================================
2056	
2057	/// Convert f32 to bf16 using simple truncation.
2058	///
2059	/// BF16 format: 1 sign + 8 exponent + 7 mantissa = 16 bits
2060	/// This simply takes the upper 16 bits of the f32 representation.
2061	///
2062	/// Note: This is a simple truncation, not round-to-nearest-even.
2063	/// For most ML workloads, the difference is negligible.
2064	///
2065	/// # Example
2066	///
2067	/// ```rust,ignore
2068	/// let f32_val: f32 = 3.14159;
2069	/// let bf16_bits: u16 = f32_to_bf16(f32_val);
2070	/// ```
2071	#[inline(always)]
2072	pub fn f32_to_bf16(val: f32) -> u16 {
2073	    // BF16 is just the upper 16 bits of f32
2074	    // f32: 1 sign + 8 exp + 23 mantissa
2075	    // bf16: 1 sign + 8 exp + 7 mantissa (truncate lower 16 bits)
2076	    (val.to_bits() >> 16) as u16
2077	}
2078	
2079	/// Convert f32 to bf16 with round-to-nearest-even.
2080	///
2081	/// This provides slightly more accurate conversion than simple truncation
2082	/// by rounding the truncated mantissa bits.
2083	#[inline(always)]
2084	pub fn f32_to_bf16_rne(val: f32) -> u16 {
2085	    let bits = val.to_bits();
2086	    // Round to nearest even: add 0x7FFF + bit 16 (round up if bit 16 is 1)
2087	    let round_bit = (bits >> 16) & 1;
2088	    let rounded = bits.wrapping_add(0x7FFF + round_bit);
2089	    (rounded >> 16) as u16
2090	}
2091	
2092	/// Pack two bf16 values (as u16) into a single u32.
2093	///
2094	/// The first bf16 goes in the lower 16 bits, the second in the upper 16 bits.
2095	/// This is used to prepare data for `stmatrix` which expects packed 16-bit values.
2096	///
2097	/// # Example
2098	///
2099	/// ```rust,ignore
2100	/// let bf16_0 = f32_to_bf16(f32_vals[0]);
2101	/// let bf16_1 = f32_to_bf16(f32_vals[1]);
2102	/// let packed = pack_bf16_pair(bf16_0, bf16_1);
2103	/// // packed is now ready for stmatrix_m8n8_x4_trans
2104	/// ```
2105	#[inline(always)]
2106	pub fn pack_bf16_pair(lo: u16, hi: u16) -> u32 {
2107	    (lo as u32) | ((hi as u32) << 16)
2108	}
2109	
2110	/// Pack two f16 values (as u16) into a single u32.
2111	///
2112	/// The first f16 goes in the lower 16 bits, the second in the upper 16 bits.
2113	#[inline(always)]
2114	pub fn pack_f16_pair(lo: u16, hi: u16) -> u32 {
2115	    (lo as u32) | ((hi as u32) << 16)
2116	}
2117	
2118	/// Convert two consecutive f32 values to bf16 and pack into u32.
2119	///
2120	/// This is a convenience function that combines conversion and packing
2121	/// for the common epilog pattern.
2122	///
2123	/// # Example
2124	///
2125	/// ```rust,ignore
2126	/// let f32_pair: [f32; 2] = [a, b];
2127	/// let packed: u32 = f32_pair_to_packed_bf16(f32_pair[0], f32_pair[1]);
2128	/// ```
2129	#[inline(always)]
2130	pub fn f32_pair_to_packed_bf16(a: f32, b: f32) -> u32 {
2131	    pack_bf16_pair(f32_to_bf16(a), f32_to_bf16(b))
2132	}

32	/// Emit movmatrix_trans_b16: in-register 8×8 matrix transpose.
33	///
34	/// Takes one u32 operand and returns one u32.
35	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
36	pub fn emit_movmatrix_trans_b16(
37	    ctx: &mut Context,
38	    body: &mir::Body,
39	    args: &[mir::Operand],
40	    destination: &mir::Place,
41	    target: &Option<usize>,
42	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
43	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
44	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
45	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
46	    loc: Location,
47	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
48	    if args.len() != 1 {
49	        return input_err!(
50	            loc.clone(),
51	            TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
52	                "movmatrix_trans_b16 expects 1 argument, got {}",
53	                args.len()
54	            ))
55	        );
56	    }
57	
58	    let (a_val, last_op) = rvalue::translate_operand(
59	        ctx,
60	        body,
61	        &args[0],
62	        value_map,
63	        block_ptr,
64	        prev_op,
65	        loc.clone(),
66	    )?;
67	
68	    let u32_ty = IntegerType::get(ctx, 32, Signedness::Unsigned);
69	
70	    let mov_op = Operation::new(
71	        ctx,
72	        MovmatrixTransB16Op::get_concrete_op_info(),
73	        vec![u32_ty.into()],
74	        vec![a_val],
75	        vec![],
76	        0,
77	    );
78	    mov_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
79	
80	    if let Some(prev) = last_op {
81	        mov_op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
82	    } else {
83	        mov_op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
84	    }
85	
86	    let result = mov_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
87	    emit_store_result_and_goto(
88	        ctx,
89	        destination,
90	        result,
91	        target,
92	        block_ptr,
93	        mov_op,
94	        value_map,
95	        block_map,
96	        loc,
97	        "movmatrix_trans_b16 call without target block",
98	    )
99	}

101	/// Extract a fixed-size Rust array into scalar SSA register values.
102	///
103	/// Constant-field extraction lowers to LLVM `extractvalue`, so no temporary
104	/// stack slot is introduced for the MMA fragments.
105	fn extract_array_registers(
106	    ctx: &mut Context,
107	    array: Value,
108	    expected_element_ty: TypeHandle,
109	    expected_len: usize,
110	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
111	    mut last_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
112	    loc: Location,
113	    fragment_name: &str,
114	) -> TranslationResult<(Vec<Value>, Ptr<Operation>)> {
115	    let array_ty = array.get_type(ctx);
116	    let valid_array = {
117	        let array_ty = array_ty.deref(ctx);
118	        array_ty
119	            .downcast_ref::<MirArrayType>()
120	            .is_some_and(|array_ty| {
121	                array_ty.size() == expected_len as u64
122	                    && array_ty.element_type() == expected_element_ty
123	            })
124	    };
125	    if !valid_array {
126	        return input_err!(
127	            loc,
128	            TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
129	                "MMA {fragment_name} fragment must be an array of {expected_len} scalar registers"
130	            ))
131	        );
132	    }
133	
134	    let mut registers = Vec::with_capacity(expected_len);
135	    for index in 0..expected_len {
136	        let extract = Operation::new(
137	            ctx,
138	            MirExtractFieldOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
139	            vec![expected_element_ty],
140	            vec![array],
141	            vec![],
142	            0,
143	        );
144	        extract.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
145	        let extract = MirExtractFieldOp::new(extract);
146	        extract.set_attr_index(ctx, FieldIndexAttr(index as u32));
147	        if let Some(previous) = last_op {
148	            extract.get_operation().insert_after(ctx, previous);
149	        } else {
150	            extract.get_operation().insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
151	        }
152	        last_op = Some(extract.get_operation());
153	        registers.push(extract.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0));
154	    }
155	
156	    Ok((registers, last_op.expect("non-empty MMA fragments")))
157	}

184	                args.len()
185	            ))
186	        );
187	    }
188	
189	    let f32_ty = FP32Type::get(ctx);
190	    let u32_ty = IntegerType::get(ctx, 32, Signedness::Unsigned);
191	
192	    let (c_array, last_op) = rvalue::translate_operand(
193	        ctx,

201	    let (c_registers, last_op) = extract_array_registers(

222	        ctx,

224	        u32_ty.into(),
225	        4,
226	        block_ptr,
227	        last_op_after,
228	        loc.clone(),
229	        "A",
230	    )?;
231	
232	    let (b_array, last_op_after) = rvalue::translate_operand(
233	        ctx,
234	        body,
235	        &args[2],
236	        value_map,
237	        block_ptr,
238	        Some(last_op),
239	        loc.clone(),
240	    )?;
241	    let (b_registers, last_op) = extract_array_registers(
242	        ctx,
243	        b_array,
244	        u32_ty.into(),
245	        2,
246	        block_ptr,
247	        last_op_after,
248	        loc.clone(),
249	        "B",
250	    )?;
251	
252	    let mut operands = c_registers;
253	    operands.extend(a_registers);
254	    operands.extend(b_registers);
255	
256	    let mma_op = Operation::new(
257	        ctx,
258	        MmaM16N8K16F32Bf16Op::get_concrete_op_info(),
259	        vec![f32_ty.into(); 4],
260	        operands,
261	        vec![],
262	        0,
263	    );
264	    mma_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
265	    mma_op.insert_after(ctx, last_op);
266	
267	    let d_registers = (0..4)
268	        .map(|index| mma_op.deref(ctx).get_result(index))
269	        .collect();
270	    let array_ty = MirArrayType::get(ctx, f32_ty.into(), 4);
271	    let d_array = Operation::new(
272	        ctx,
273	        MirConstructArrayOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
274	        vec![array_ty.into()],
275	        d_registers,
276	        vec![],
277	        0,
278	    );
279	    d_array.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
280	    d_array.insert_after(ctx, mma_op);
281	    let result = d_array.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
282	
283	    emit_store_result_and_goto(
284	        ctx,
285	        destination,
286	        result,
287	        target,
288	        block_ptr,
289	        d_array,
290	        value_map,
291	        block_map,
292	        loc,
293	        "mma_m16n8k16_f32_bf16 call without target block",
294	    )
295	}
